Your Party is Local

Today your Indiana Republican Party has never been stronger. We hold every statewide elected office, both U.S. Senate seats, seven U.S. House seats and supermajorities in the Indiana General Assembly.

But did you also know that we hold 89% of all county commissioner seats?

That’s because your Republican Party isn’t just winning elections statewide. We’re winning local elections in communities throughout Indiana. And that’s a direct credit to our local Republican Party teams in all 92 counties and our grassroots Republicans who are out there working hard every day for our party and our candidates.

And while elections may still be months out, this is a busy time for your local Republican team. For many counties, their biggest fundraiser of the year is approaching, the annual Lincoln Day Dinner....and they’ve got some great food, great camaraderie, and really great featured speakers planned for you.

You can hear from folks like your local congressman, Senator Todd Young, Senator Mike Braun, Governor Eric Holcomb or Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch. I’m the keynote at some events – and some counties are bringing in big names like Wyoming Congresswoman Liz Cheney and NRA president Oliver North!

If you’ve never been to a Lincoln Day Dinner, now’s the year to attend! Check out the list of scheduled event (more still to come!) below.

-Kyle Hupfer

Half Time + Fox News

It’s half time for the 2019 Indiana legislative session! While the bills are switching between the House and Senate, Governor Eric Holcomb’s Next Level Agenda bills are continuing to move through the legislative process – including bills to:
* Exempt military pensions from state income tax
* Improve school safety
* Modernize Indiana’s economic development tools
* Help Hoosiers build skills to fill high-wage jobs
* Increase tax credits for teachers
* Streamline state boards and commissions

And when it comes to helping folks build the skills needed to fill great jobs, Governor Holcomb is taking Indiana’s success story to the nation!

Governor Holcomb has been appointed to serve on President Donald Trump’s new American Workforce Policy Advisory Board, and will advise the Trump Administration on ways to expand the Indiana model.

He’ll be on Fox News this Sunday during the 9 p.m. ET hour talking about it...so make sure to tune in! Learn more about it here! (https://holcombforindiana.com/foxnews-332019/?mc_cid=4e7d9f2b63&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

Get to Know New Albany Mayoral Candidate Mark Seabrook!

It’s a municipal election year in Indiana! We have Hoosier Republicans statewide who have stepped forward to run for mayor this year – including local businessman Mark Seabrook, who is running for mayor of New Albany in southern Indiana.

As a former New Albany City Council member and member of the Floyd County Commissioners, he has a plan and vision for New Albany. Check it out!

Q: What makes New Albany unique?
A: New Albany has a spirit unique to itself, but is located just across the bridge from Louisville and all its cultural, educational, workforce and transportation opportunities.

Q: What are your biggest priorities if elected?
A: We’ve got four big priorities, including –
1. We will put neighborhoods first. We will create, with your help, a cohesive, coordinated plan to revitalize our neighborhoods. We must make our city more livable.
2. We will make transparency the foundation of all we do.
3. We will look for ways for government to work smarter.
4. We will be one team in city government.

Q: What’s your vision for New Albany in ten years?
A: I look forward to seeing neighborhoods that have been revived and that are serving as the community foundations I know they can be. We’ll have a city government that is operating at the next level, known for its ease of accessibility, transparency, inclusion, and smart spending.

Q: What inspired you to run for New Albany’s highest office?
A: There is room for big improvement versus how our city operates today. Plus, we have the best team running for city council since the 1960s!

Are you a Republican mayoral candidate (without a Republican primary challenger) who would like to be featured in an upcoming edition of The Party Line? Contact us at info@indiana.gop (mailto:info@indiana.gop) , and we’ll be in touch soon!

Washington Township Does Lincoln Day Brunch!

Not all Lincoln Days have to be dinners!

Last week, the Washington Township Republicans in Marion County held their own Lincoln Day Brunch, and attracted a great crowd, including mayoral candidate and Indiana Senator Jim Merritt and Mark Miles, President & CEO Hulman & Company. Great work, Washington Township Republicans!

Next week, be sure to check out Lincoln Days in Miami and Boone counties, and in Pike Township in Marion County!

subject: Passing along Kate's message

body:

| | |

|
---
---
| |
---
| Derek, One of the first things I did as Chairman of the NHGOP was reach out to former Cheshire County Chairman Kate Day to utilize her leadership in rebuilding the ranks of our town committees and city ward captains. I am glad Kate has accepted and the Executive Committee confirmed her appointment to this critical role. Kate has already started working to identify new local committee leadership throughout the Granite State. I know with Kate at the helm, she will get us to our goal of 100 new town committees and 30 city ward captains for this cycle. Please see Kate's note below. If you're interested in getting involved in your community, please reach out to her through her email, [Kate@NHGOP.org](mailto:kate@nhgop.org)! Sincerely, Stephen Stepanek NHGOP Chairman

---
---
| |
---
|
---
| |
---
| I am very excited to be working with all our county and city groups across the state to build new town committees and revitalize inactive ones. Grassroots connections at the local level will be the building blocks of our 2020 victory. Our NHGOP Chairman, Stephen Stepanek, has challenged us all with a goal of 100 new town committees across the state, and to increase our membership at all levels. **We can do this. Let's start NOW!** This coming month, New Hampshire will have Town Meetings across the state. Pay attention to those who are like-minded. Times today call for being **BOLD! ** Introduce yourself and ask some folks to join you at the next town or county meeting. If you don't have a town committee, ask people to join you in forming a new GOP town committee. We will walk you through it. Sometimes, people just have to be asked. It is a great opportunity to spread our Republican values to those who are ready to walk away from the Democrats, who are now boldly becoming the Socialist Party. We will be developing new ideas to network with closed email groups for towns to share ideas on events, fundraising, recruitment, messaging, best practices, and information on local and legislative issues. Training will be in the works to give you the tools for a successful town committee, new or old. It will be important to hear from you, your ideas, and the challenges you face in your town. Together we can learn the best way to reach out to voters, get Republicans to the polls, and how to change independent hearts and minds to join us. Heck, with the outlandish policies Democrats are supporting, we may get some reasonable Democrats to join us! #WalkAway! Our NHGOP Committee on Town Committees will be updating the Town Committee Starter Kit and identifying towns needing help or a new committee. We can be creative in finding the best way to build Republican efforts in your area. No town is too small to be a part of the fight to win in 2020! Please feel free to contact me at [Kate@NHGOP.org](mailto:Kate@NHGOP.org) or call, 603.363.9391. Sincerely, Kate Day Chair of the Committee on Town Committees
---
---
| |
---
| New Hampshire Republican State Committee

subject: Republicans are attacking human rights and healthcare in one bill
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
Peter,

Tennessee Republicans are so hellbent on stripping LGBTQ couples of their right to marry, that they're willing to sacrifice $9.4 billion in federal funding, which would decimate TennCare and safety-net benefits for over 2 million Tennesseans.
* **_Tennessean (2/25/19)_ : 'Natural marriage' bill could put state in jeopardy of losing $9.4 billion in federal funds**

**Because the Natural Marriage Defense Act would deny spousal healthcare benefits to same-sex couples, Tennessee would be in violation of federal law and would have to forfeit its $7.4 billion in federal funding for TennCare.** This proposal comes after Republican leadership has already cost Tennessee $7 billion in federal funds by refusing to expand Medicaid under the Affordable Care Act.

The Tennessee Department of Human Services says it would lose $2 billion in federal funding for the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program and the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families, programs used by over 1 million Tennessee families.

Tennessee Democrats are combat these efforts both inside the State House and on the campaign trial.

name: BetoORourke.com
subject: How we move forward on immigration policy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR TEXAS
body:
If we want to make real progress and achieve actual change then we can’t
just say what we’re against, we have to say what we’re for.

As Donald Trump pushes for a 2,000-mile wall on our southern border and
spreads lies about immigrants, it’s on us to offer something
better. That’s why Beto outlined ten essential immigration, border, and
bilateral policies that match the reality on the ground and reflect our
values as a country.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/immigration/?t=1001&akid=24960%2E444211%2EGUH0t7 ]Can you sign your name in support of Beto’s ten points below? We want
to show how many people are with us on this kind of positive, ambitious,
values-based vision for immigration policy.

Add your name »

1. Extend citizenship to the more than one million Dreamers in this
country. Not only those who are in our classrooms but those who are
teaching in our classrooms; those who are keeping our country safe
around the world tonight; those who contribute more to our communities
than they’ll ever take.
2. Give permanent legal protection and a path to citizenship to their
parents, the original Dreamers.
3. Bring millions more out of the shadows and on a path to citizenship by
ensuring that they register with the government and gain status to
legally work, pay taxes and contribute even more to our country’s
success.
4. Make us safer and more secure. Significantly reduce illegal drug
trafficking and stop human trafficking by investing in infrastructure,
technology and personnel at our ports of entry. The ports that connect
us with Mexico are where the vast majority of everything and everyone
that ever comes into our country crosses.
5. Increase the visa caps so that we match our opportunities and needs
(for work, for education, for investment, for innovation, for family
reunification) to the number of people we allow into this country.
Ensure that those who want to work in jobs that we can’t fill can
legally come here and legally return to their home country.
6. Fully accept our opportunity and responsibility under our asylum laws
to welcome those whose own governments can no longer protect
them — including women fleeing abusive relationships.
7. Address visa overstays (which accounts for the majority of
undocumented immigration) through better tracking of and notification
to visa holders (a first step could be text message reminders) and
fully harmonizing our entry-exit systems with Mexico and Canada (when
a visa holder exits the U.S. and enters Mexico, we will then know that
they have left the U.S.; currently, if they leave through a land port
of entry we literally have no clue if they are still here or have
returned to their country of origin).
8. Make Latin America and specifically Central America a top foreign
policy priority — stop relegating it to second-tier status — invest
the time, talent and resources to assist in the development of the
domestic institutions that will allow these countries to thrive and
offer their citizens protection and economic opportunity. It is the
only long-term solution to the number of asylum seekers and refugees
coming to this country.
9. End the global war on drugs. An imprisonment- and interdiction-first
approach has not worked, has accelerated the erosion of civil society
in much of Latin America and has militarized a public health issue to
the detriment of all concerned.
10. Speak with respect and dignity when referring to our fellow human
beings who happen to be immigrants and asylum seekers, who in so many
cases are doing exactly what we would do if presented with the same
threats and opportunities. No more “invasions”, “animals”, “rapists
and criminals”, “floods”, “crisis” — dehumanizing rhetoric leads to
dehumanizing policies. We cannot sacrifice our humanity in the name of
security — or we risk losing both.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/immigration/?t=1002&akid=24960%2E444211%2EGUH0t7 ]After you’ve added your name, we want you to share these ideas with as
many people as possible. Forward the email, or share the sign-on page on
social media.

Together, we will get past the lies and fear. We’ll put forth policies
that focus on the facts, and dignify the humanity of every person living
here. And in the end, we will have a stronger, safer, more successful
country.

Thanks for signing on in support of Beto’s 10 points today.

- Team Beto

Contribute to Beto's campaign: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beto-email?refcode=em20190302footer&t=1003&refcode2=24960_444211_GUH0t7&akid=24960%2E444211%2EGUH0t7

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betofortexas.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1004&akid=24960%2E444211%2EGUH0t7

PAID FOR BY BETO FOR TEXAS
P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923

body:
This week, the White House Council of Economic Advisers reported that America’s economy achieved 3 percent growth for the first time in 13 years... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=844a2003ba&e=3ee4985eea)

| **_REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [Thank You, Tax Reform](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e2f696d7c0&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Wall Street Journal_
This week, the White House Council of Economic Advisers reported that America’s economy achieved 3 percent growth for the first time in 13 years in 2018. “The American economy is a tremendous engine of prosperity when politicians get out of the way, and for proof look no further than Thursday’s report on fourth-quarter growth,” _The Wall Street Journal_ editorial board writes. “Tax reform and deregulation, take a bow.”

**More** : [2018—A Banner Year for the U.S. Economy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b3c527f431&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [President Trump Thanks Fiat Chrysler for ‘Coming Back to the USA’](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d65cde2feb&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Detroit Free Press_
On Tuesday, Fiat Chrysler announced that Detroit, Michigan, “will get a Jeep factory, the Motor City’s first new auto assembly plant in a generation, as part of a $4.5 billion manufacturing expansion that will mean nearly 6,500 new jobs,” Amy Huschka reports. With jobs coming back to the Midwest thanks to the promise of the new U.S.–Mexico–Canada Agreement, President Trump praised the move: “Thank you Fiat Chrysler. They are all coming back to the USA, it’s where the action is!”
---
|
---
| [In Hanoi, Trump Won By Walking Away](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e97ad3d704&e=3ee4985eea)
-_New York Post_
“Sometimes you have to walk, and this was just one of those times,” President Donald J. Trump said as he prepared to leave Vietnam following his summit with North Korea’s Kim Jong Un this week. Good for the President, the _New York Post_ editorial board says. “Just what we expected in noting the other day that ‘his history suggests he’s always willing to walk away from a deal that’s not good enough.’”
---
|
---
| [America’s Students Deserve Freedom to Choose Their Education Options](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b4a1c93c07&e=3ee4985eea)
-_USA Today_
Secretary of Education Betsy DeVos, Sen. Ted Cruz (R-TX), and Rep. Bradley Byrne (R-AL) write too many American children are being denied a great education—and that the government’s outdated approach isn’t helping. “That’s why on Thursday we [put] forward a historic investment in America’s students: the Education Freedom Scholarships and Opportunity Act.”
---
|
---
| [Pence Hits Venezuela with New Sanctions, Promises ‘Even Stronger’ Measures Ahead](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=918145cc10&e=3ee4985eea)
-NBC News
“Vice President Mike Pence on Monday announced new sanctions against Venezuelan government officials and called on allies to freeze the assets of the state-owned oil company after a deadly weekend in which U.S. aid was blocked from entering the crisis-stricken country,” Carmen Sesin reports. While in Columbia this week, the Vice President met with Venezuelan interim President Juan Guaido, whom President Trump officially recognized as Venezuela’s head of state in January.
---
|
---
| [Trump Slams Senate Democrats on Born Alive Bill: ‘One of the Most Shocking Votes in the History of Congress’](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3db5fcd7b3&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Daily Caller_
“President Donald Trump reacted on Twitter Monday evening after the Born-Alive Survivors Protection Act, which would have ensured that babies who survive abortions are given medical attention, failed to pass the Senate,” Evie Fordham reports. “This will be remembered as one of the most shocking votes in the history of Congress,” the President tweeted.
---
|
---
| [Trump Praises Purchases by Vietnamese Airlines](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bd52714cee&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Washington Examiner_
“The White House on Wednesday praised airlines in Vietnam for signing agreements to purchase more than $20 billion in U.S.-built planes and technology. President Trump and Vietnamese President Nguyen Phu Trong attended a signing ceremony that involved trade deals between a number of airline companies based in the two countries,” Zachary Halaschak writes.
---

name: Kamala Harris
subject: We did it
body:
Thanks to you and your support, we reached our important February fundraising goal. With
your help, we’re building a strong campaign from the ground up.

Yesterday, we spent time on the campaign trail in Las Vegas -- of course,
we had to stop for some tacos and conversations with Nevadans.

Then, I joined Star Jones for a conversation at the Black Enterprise Women
of Power Summit. My main takeaway: leadership should not be about using
your power to belittle and bully. It should be about lifting people up.

We then headed to our Las Vegas town hall, where more than 700 Nevadans
joined us for a conversation about the big challenges facing our country
right now -- and how we all must come together at this inflection point in
our nation’s history to do what is right.

We spoke about combating rising health care costs and what it will take to
pass Medicare for All. How we are fighting for an America where you only
have to work one job to pay the bills, where hard work is rewarded, and
where any worker can join a union.

And I spoke directly to a young girl in the audience who wondered what
it’s like to run for president. I told her that running for president is
very uplifting because I get to meet the angels who walk among us: the
people who could be doing a million other things but they choose to come
to a town hall or get engaged.

Your contributions are making it possible for us to build a winning
campaign -- and I am so grateful for your support.

For The People,

— Kamala

clean_body: Thanks to you and your support, we reached our important February fundraising goal. With your help, we’re building a strong campaign from the ground up. Yesterday, we spent time on the campaign trail in Las Vegas -- of course, we had to stop for some tacos and conversations with Nevadans. Then, I joined Star Jones for a conversation at the Black Enterprise Women of Power Summit. My main takeaway: leadership should not be about using your power to belittle and bully. It should be about lifting people up. We then headed to our Las Vegas town hall, where more than 700 Nevadans joined us for a conversation about the big challenges facing our country right now -- and how we all must come together at this inflection point in our nation’s history to do what is right. We spoke about combating rising health care costs and what it will take to pass Medicare for All. How we are fighting for an America where you only have to work one job to pay the bills, where hard work is rewarded, and where any worker can join a union. And I spoke directly to a young girl in the audience who wondered what it’s like to run for president. I told her that running for president is very uplifting because I get to meet the angels who walk among us: the people who could be doing a million other things but they choose to come to a town hall or get engaged. Your contributions are making it possible for us to build a winning campaign -- and I am so grateful for your support.
